title: aristolochia ceresensis var. lingua
intro: Aristolochia ceresensis var. lingua, also known as the Tongue Aristolochia, is a variety of the Aristolochia plant, a flowering plant native to certain regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antidote for snake bites. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
